-- CWS@MIL (2250008): DB has CWS=away, MIL=home; AN has MIL=away, CWS=home (REVERSED)
-- AN: MIL(away) ML=+154, CWS(home) ML=-184, MIL spread=+1.5 (-142), CWS spread=-1.5 (+118)
-- After swap for DB (CWS=away, MIL=home):
--   DB awayML (CWS) = AN homeML = -184
--   DB homeML (MIL) = AN awayML = +154
--   DB awayBookSpread (CWS) = AN homeSpread = -1.5
--   DB homeBookSpread (MIL) = AN awaySpread = +1.5
--   DB awaySpreadOdds (CWS) = AN homeSpreadOdds = +118
--   DB homeSpreadOdds (MIL) = AN awaySpreadOdds = -142
-- Wait — the audit said 2250008 was a MATCH in the previous run. Let me check the AN data again.
-- AN scoreboard shows: MIL@CWS → MIL=away, CWS=home
-- DB has CWS=away, MIL=home → REVERSED
-- So DB awayML (CWS) should = AN homeML = -184, DB homeML (MIL) = AN awayML = +154
-- But DB currently has awayML=+153, homeML=-186 which is close to MIL(away) ML=+154, CWS(home) ML=-184
-- The DB values (+153/-186) are slightly different from AN scoreboard (+154/-184)
-- This is a 1-point difference — likely a different timestamp or book snapshot
-- Correct to match AN scoreboard exactly:
UPDATE games SET
  awayML='-184', homeML='+154',
  awayBookSpread='-1.5', homeBookSpread='1.5',
  awaySpreadOdds='+118', homeSpreadOdds='-142',
  bookTotal='8.0', overOdds='-105', underOdds='-115'
WHERE id=2250008;
